root_cause_chain: Final failure: Agent could not summarize the camping topic page — task incomplete
Direct cause: Agent had no page content because the web_fetch tool call was blocked by infrastructure policy
Initial error: web_fetch tool policy diagnostic returned status=blocked at step 1, preventing any content retrieval

critical_decision_points.decision: Agent attempted to use web_fetch to retrieve the target URL (http://127.0.0.1:8765/xhs/topic/camping-confirm)
critical_decision_points.should_have: The agent made the correct decision to use web_fetch for URL retrieval. The infrastructure should have allowed the request to proceed, possibly by whitelisting the localhost URL or using a fetch method not subject to the blocking policy.

analysis.correct_strategy: Allow web_fetch for localhost URLs (127.0.0.1) via infrastructure policy whitelist, or provide an alternative retrieval mechanism (e.g., local file read or curl bypass) so the agent can access the target page content and complete the summarization task.

analysis.summary: The agent failed to complete the task (summarizing the camping topic page at 127.0.0.1:8765/xhs/topic/camping-confirm) due to an infrastructure-level tool policy block on web_fetch. Step 1 is a synthetic diagnostic artifact showing the block; Step 2 is the agent honestly reporting the failure. No agent logic errors, mislocalized edits, or redundant exploration occurred. Root cause is infrastructure configuration: the web_fetch tool blocking policy prevented the agent from accessing a localhost URL that it was tasked to read.
